coverage: 100% of reachable node types across the 296-group, 2,413-node closure, including its six-object pipe structure collection.
validation: The authored six-way pipe layout, generated sleeves, SDF joint shell, dual-mesh smoothing, and structure toggle run in-browser. Collection Info and the instance-domain For Each zone preserve Blender's full affine transforms and direct component-join semantics. The imported six-object structure is exact at 4,968 / 4,758. Generation_0 is exact at 10,578 / 10,326 and Generation_1 is exact at 18,060 / 18,060, with all 28,638 realized hull positions bit-identical to Blender by index. All 15,600 samples in the first Mesh To SDF / Volume Cube FloatGrid are bit-identical to Blender. A native OpenVDB 13 probe of that captured grid proves GN-VM's resampled FloatGrid is also bit-identical (FNV-1a b538f595de36d9d6). Passing the authored zero threshold verbatim makes both native OpenVDB and GN-VM emit 3,478 / 3,476 for the first surface, advancing the former 3,470 / 3,468 browser result; the in-graph Blender surface remains 3,496 / 3,494. The downstream second remesh is still 3,362 / 3,360 versus Blender 3,308 / 3,306, producing 11,226 / 8,118 versus 11,162 / 8,064 with exact authored bounds. The remaining work is the 18-face difference between Blender's in-graph sparse tree and the independently reconstructed bit-identical dense field, followed by the second repeat—not collection extraction, SDF evaluation, generated instances, or convex-hull construction.
